---
title: Math 2710 Schedule
---

## Schedule

<style>
#sched { border-collapse: collapse; }
#sched th, #sched td { border: 1px solid #ccc; padding: 6px 12px; }
#sched tr.week-end td { border-bottom: 2px solid #555; }
#sched thead th { border-bottom: 2px solid #555; }
#sched td:first-child { white-space: nowrap; }
</style>

<table id="sched" style="width: 100%">
<colgroup>
  <col style="width: 10%">
  <col style="width: 15%">
  <col style="width: 75%">
</colgroup>
<thead>
<tr><th>Date</th><th>Sections</th><th>Topics</th></tr>
</thead>
<tbody>
<tr><td>Tu 09/01</td>
  <td>§1.1</td>
  <td><a href="notes/Chapter1/Ch1.1a.html">Sets, elements, and set notation</a>; <a href="notes/Chapter1/Ch1.1b.html">set builder notation</a></td></tr>
<tr class="week-end"><td>Th 09/03</td>
  <td>§1.2–1.3</td>
  <td><a href="notes/Chapter1/Ch1.2.html">Cartesian products</a>; <a href="notes/Chapter1/Ch1.3a.html">subsets</a>; <a href="notes/Chapter1/Ch1.3b.html">counting subsets</a></td></tr>
<tr><td>Tu 09/08</td>
  <td>§1.4–1.5</td>
  <td><a href="notes/Chapter1/Ch1.4.html">Power sets</a>; <a href="notes/Chapter1/Ch1.5.html">union, intersection, and difference</a></td></tr>
<tr class="week-end"><td>Th 09/10</td>
  <td>§1.6–1.8</td>
  <td><a href="notes/Chapter1/Ch1.6.html">Complement</a>; <a href="notes/Chapter1/Ch1.7.html">Venn diagrams</a>; <a href="notes/Chapter1/Ch1.8.html">indexed sets</a></td></tr>
<tr><td>Tu 09/15</td>
  <td>§2.1–2.3</td>
  <td><a href="notes/Chapter2/Ch2.1.html">Statements and truth values</a>; <a href="notes/Chapter2/Ch2.2.html">connectives</a>; <a href="notes/Chapter2/Ch2.3.html">conditional statements</a></td></tr>
<tr class="week-end"><td>Th 09/17</td>
  <td>§2.4–2.6</td>
  <td><a href="notes/Chapter2/Ch2.4.html">Biconditionals</a>; <a href="notes/Chapter2/Ch2.5.html">truth tables</a>; <a href="notes/Chapter2/Ch2.6.html">logical equivalence</a></td></tr>
<tr><td>Tu 09/22</td>
  <td>§2.7–2.8</td>
  <td><a href="notes/Chapter2/Ch2.7.html">Quantifiers</a>; <a href="notes/Chapter2/Ch2.7b.html">paired quantifiers</a>; <a href="notes/Chapter2/Ch2.8.html">implications with open sentences</a></td></tr>
<tr class="week-end"><td>Th 09/24</td>
  <td>§2.9–2.11</td>
  <td><a href="notes/Chapter2/Ch2.9.html">Translating into logic</a>; <a href="notes/Chapter2/Ch2.10.html">negations</a>; <a href="notes/Chapter2/Ch2.11.html">deduction rules</a></td></tr>
<tr><td>Tu 09/29</td>
  <td>Review</td>
  <td></td></tr>
<tr class="week-end"><td>Th 10/01</td>
  <td>First Midterm</td>
  <td></td></tr>
<tr><td>Tu 10/06</td>
  <td>§3.1–3.2</td>
  <td><a href="notes/Chapter3/Ch3.1.html">Multiplication principle</a>; <a href="notes/Chapter3/Ch3.2.html">addition principle</a></td></tr>
<tr class="week-end"><td>Th 10/08</td>
  <td>§3.3–3.5</td>
  <td><a href="notes/Chapter3/Ch3.3.html">Permutations</a>; <a href="notes/Chapter3/Ch3.4.html">combinations</a>; <a href="notes/Chapter3/Ch3.5.html">Pascal's triangle and binomial theorem</a></td></tr>
<tr><td>Tu 10/13</td>
  <td>§4.1–4.2</td>
  <td><a href="notes/Chapter4/Ch4.1.html">Theorems and definitions</a>; <a href="notes/Chapter4/Ch4.2.html">division algorithm</a></td></tr>
<tr class="week-end"><td>Th 10/15</td>
  <td>§4.3</td>
  <td><a href="notes/Chapter4/Ch4.3a.html">Direct proofs</a>; <a href="notes/Chapter4/Ch4.3b.html">examples</a></td></tr>
<tr><td>Tu 10/20</td>
  <td>§4.3–4.4</td>
  <td><a href="notes/Chapter4/Ch4.3d.html">Using definitions in proofs</a>; <a href="notes/Chapter4/Ch4.3e.html">more direct proofs</a>; <a href="notes/Chapter4/Ch4.4.html">proof by cases</a></td></tr>
<tr class="week-end"><td>Th 10/22</td>
  <td>§5.1–5.2</td>
  <td><a href="notes/Chapter5/Ch5.1.html">Proof by contrapositive</a>; <a href="notes/Chapter5/Ch5.2a.html">congruences</a>; <a href="notes/Chapter5/Ch5.2b.html">congruence arithmetic</a></td></tr>
<tr><td>Tu 10/27</td>
  <td>§6.1, §7.1</td>
  <td><a href="notes/Chapter6/Ch6.1.html">Proof by contradiction</a>; <a href="notes/Chapter6/Ch6.1b.html">Euclid's proof of infinitely many primes</a>; <a href="notes/Chapter7/Ch7.1.html">if-and-only-if proofs</a></td></tr>
<tr class="week-end"><td>Th 10/29</td>
  <td>§7.3–7.4</td>
  <td><a href="notes/Chapter7/Ch7.3.html">Existence proofs</a>; <a href="notes/Chapter7/Ch7.3b.html">Euclid's algorithm</a>; <a href="notes/Chapter7/Ch7.3c.html">uniqueness proofs</a>; <a href="notes/Chapter7/Ch7.4.html">constructive and non-constructive proofs</a></td></tr>
<tr><td>Tu 11/03</td>
  <td>Review</td>
  <td></td></tr>
<tr class="week-end"><td>Th 11/05</td>
  <td>Second Midterm</td>
  <td></td></tr>
<tr><td>Tu 11/10</td>
  <td>§8.1–8.3</td>
  <td><a href="notes/Chapter8/Ch8.1.html">Proving set membership</a>; <a href="notes/Chapter8/Ch8.2.html">set inclusion proofs</a>; <a href="notes/Chapter8/Ch8.3.html">set equality proofs</a></td></tr>
<tr class="week-end"><td>Th 11/12</td>
  <td>§9.1–9.3</td>
  <td><a href="notes/Chapter9/Ch9.1a.html">Counterexamples</a>; <a href="notes/Chapter9/Ch9.1b.html">disproving universal statements</a>; <a href="notes/Chapter9/Ch9.2-3.html">disproving existence claims</a></td></tr>
<tr><td>Tu 11/17</td>
  <td>§10.1</td>
  <td><a href="notes/Chapter10/Ch10.intro.html">Introduction to induction</a>; <a href="notes/Chapter10/Ch10.1.html">induction proofs</a>; <a href="notes/Chapter10/Ch10.1b.html">more examples</a></td></tr>
<tr class="week-end"><td>Th 11/19</td>
  <td>§10.2–10.5</td>
  <td><a href="notes/Chapter10/Ch10.2.html">Strong induction</a>; <a href="notes/Chapter10/Ch10.3.html">strong induction continued</a>; <a href="notes/Chapter10/Ch10.4.html">fundamental theorem of arithmetic</a>; <a href="notes/Chapter10/Ch10.5.html">Fibonacci numbers</a>; <a href="notes/Chapter10/Ch10.wellordering.html">well-ordering principle</a></td></tr>
<tr><td>Tu 11/24</td>
  <td>Thanksgiving Break</td>
  <td></td></tr>
<tr class="week-end"><td>Th 11/26</td>
  <td>Thanksgiving Break</td>
  <td></td></tr>
<tr><td>Tu 12/01</td>
  <td>§11.1–11.2</td>
  <td><a href="notes/Chapter11/Ch11.1.html">Relations</a>; <a href="notes/Chapter11/Ch11.2.html">reflexive, symmetric, and transitive properties</a></td></tr>
<tr class="week-end"><td>Th 12/03</td>
  <td>§11.3–11.6</td>
  <td><a href="notes/Chapter11/Ch11.3.html">Equivalence relations</a>; <a href="notes/Chapter11/Ch11.4.html">partitions</a>; <a href="notes/Chapter11/Ch11.5.html">integers mod n</a>; <a href="notes/Chapter11/Ch11.6.html">relations between sets</a></td></tr>
<tr><td>Tu 12/08</td>
  <td>§12.1–12.2</td>
  <td><a href="notes/Chapter12/Ch12.1.html">Functions</a>; <a href="notes/Chapter12/Ch12.2.html">injective, surjective, and bijective functions</a></td></tr>
<tr class="week-end"><td>Th 12/10</td>
  <td>§12.3–12.6</td>
  <td><a href="notes/Chapter12/Ch12.3.html">Pigeonhole principle</a>; <a href="notes/Chapter12/Ch12.4.html">composition</a>; <a href="notes/Chapter12/Ch12.5.html">inverse functions</a>; <a href="notes/Chapter12/Ch12.6.html">image and preimage</a></td></tr>
</tbody>
</table>
